_description: Golden corpus of IAM policy fixtures for regression testing the review/classification/detection flow. Each entry documents the scenario being tested, the actions in the fixture, and the key expected outputs that regression tests assert against.
_schema_note: evidence_layers: 'catalog' = static HIGH/MEDIUM_RISK_ACTIONS sets; 'classification' = reviewed action_classification.yaml records; 'composite' = multi-capability pattern rules in composite_detections.yaml

priv_escalation_ec2.scenario: Privilege escalation via EC2 compute — complete chain: role delegation + compute launch

expected_fix.note: Both actions are classified/high — fix gate passes for both; all actions removed so placeholder is inserted.

priv_escalation_lambda.scenario: Privilege escalation via Lambda serverless — role delegation + function creation

expected_fix.note: Both actions are classified/high — gate passes; placeholder inserted.

policy_modification.scenario: Direct IAM policy takeover — policy-modification capability alone is sufficient for COMP-003

credential_and_secret_read.scenario: Credential exfiltration — composite COMP-004 fires but neither action is in HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS, so fix does not auto-remove. Composite detection adds value beyond individual findings.

expected_fix.note: Neither action is in HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS — fix has nothing to remove. Actions remain in output policy. Manual review is the only recourse.

unknown_action_only.scenario: Unknown action not present in any evidence layer — proves system does not over-classify unreviewed actions

composite.note: No classified capabilities — no composite rule can fire.

expected_fix.note: Not in HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS — fix gate never evaluates it. Action is left untouched with no manual_review note.

data_read_no_composite.scenario: Data-read-only capabilities — COMP-009 and COMP-010 require additional capabilities not present, so no composite rule fires

composite.note: Only data-read-sensitive is present. COMP-009 needs secret-read also. COMP-010 needs data-write-sensitive also. No rule fires.

expected_rule_findings.note: rds:CopyDBSnapshot is in MEDIUM_RISK_ACTIONS (R004). dynamodb:Scan may produce a context-dependent R004 on unrestricted Resource.

expected_fix.note: Neither action is in HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS — fix retains both.

mixed_classified_and_unknown.scenario: Mixed policy: one classified/high action and one unknown action. Fix removes the classified action; unknown is left untouched.

composite.note: Only privilege-delegation is confirmed. No composite rule requires privilege-delegation alone.

expected_fix.note: iam:PassRole is classified/high — removed. svc:FutureAction is not in HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS — left alone with no gate note.

not_applicable_gate.scenario: Action in HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS but classified as not-applicable — R003 fires (catalog match) but fix gate blocks removal (reviewed classification overrides).

composite.note: not-applicable classification means is_confirmed_risky=False — no capabilities contribute to composite matching.

expected_rule_findings.note: R003 fires because kms:DescribeKey is in the static HIGH_RISK_ACTIONS set. Classification status is not checked by the rule engine.

expected_fix.note: Fix gate checks classification and finds not-applicable — removal withheld. Action remains in output policy. Manual review note explains reason.
